  • Home

  • GitHub
v1.0 — Now Available

Your Terminal,
Anywhere.

A lightweight, mobile-optimized web terminal controller that mirrors and controls local shell sessions from your phone — zero cloud dependencies, fully self-hosted.

Get Started View on GitHub
≤150ms Input→Output Latency
≤40MB Memory (5 shells)
0 Cloud Dependencies
tether — bash
$ ls -la
total 48
drwxr-xr-x 8 user 4096 Apr 3
-rw-r--r-- 1 user 1234 Apr 3
$ █
Features

Everything you need for
mobile terminal control

📱

Mobile-First UI

Touch-optimized, responsive xterm.js interface designed specifically for phones and tablets.

🔐

Password Auth

Argon2id hashed passwords with session cookies, rate limiting, and 7-day expiry.

🖥️

Multi-PTY

Spawn and manage multiple terminal sessions simultaneously with a swipeable drawer.

⚡

Real-Time

WebSocket-based streaming with ≤150ms input→output latency on your LAN.

🎛️

Preset Commands

Quick-tap buttons for common commands — ls, top, df, free — one tap away.

📦

Single Binary

Frontend embedded via rust-embed. No npm, no external dependencies, just run.

Quick Start

Up and running in 30 seconds

1

Install

git clone https://github.com/ssnelavala-masstcs/tether.git cd tether cargo build --release
2

Run

./target/release/tether serve --password "secret" --allow-lan
3

Access from Phone

Open the printed URL (e.g., http://192.168.1.100:8080) on your phone's browser, enter the password, and start controlling your terminal.
